2023 · HS Playoff Highlights
THE GLORY DAYS
1923 · Iowa State Jack Trice — the only college football stadium named after an African American player
1970s · Nebraska Coach Bob Devaney — built the Nebraska dynasty, won 2 national titles, and gave a Wyoming QB named Jim Walden his first coaching job.
🏈
1980s · Washington State
1980s · Washington State Mark Rypien #17 with Coach Jim Walden — before Rypien became Super Bowl MVP with the Washington Redskins
1980s · WSU Basketball Coach George Raveling — WSU basketball legend and Murray Walden's neighbor in Pullman. Murray mowed his lawn every summer.
1960s · Wyoming Cowboys Jim Walden — All-American QB & NFL draft pick. His son never saw him play until he called Wyoming to digitize 3 games. That birthday gift became GloryDays.
🏆
The Burrow Family · Amory, MS
The Burrow Family · Amory, MS Dot Burrow scored a state-record 82 pts in one HS game. When told Kobe scored 81 in the NBA, Joe said: “Yeah, but Kobe’s still one short of my grandma.”
Every era. Every team. Every memory — preserved forever.

Video Archive — Wyoming Cowboys Football · 1959
📽️
HISTORIC ARCHIVE — RECENTLY UPLOADED
Four original 16mm game films from the 1959 Wyoming Cowboys football season — now digitized and available on GloryDays for the first time. These films capture a complete October 1959 stretch of Cowboy football, preserved for players, families, and fans.
Wyoming Cowboys · Football · 1959
Wyoming vs Utah State
Skyline Conference · October 3, 1959
📍 War Memorial Stadium, Laramie, Wyoming

Print & Archives — Wyoming Cowboys · 1959
📰
NEW FEATURE — PRINT MEDIA ARCHIVE
Upload newspaper clippings, game programs, scorecards & photos. Our AI reads the text, finds athlete names, and alerts matching members — even when no video exists. All items save to your personal locker alongside video and audio.
📰
NEWSPAPER CLIPPING
Drag & drop or tap to upload
📋
GAME PROGRAM
Drag & drop or tap to upload
📷
PHOTOGRAPH
Drag & drop or tap to upload
⚡ AI READS YOUR UPLOAD
Our OCR engine extracts all text from your upload. Named Entity Recognition then scans for athlete names and cross-references them against registered members. Any member whose name is found gets a personal alert — even if no video exists for that game.
SAMPLE ARCHIVE ITEMS · 1959 SEASON
📰
LARAMIE DAILY BOOMERANG
October 4, 1959
NEWSPAPER
"Cowboys Defeat Utah State 21–14 in War Memorial Thriller"
Game recap — Wyoming Cowboys vs Utah State, Oct. 3, 1959. Mentions: Jim Walden, Bob Grisham, Dale Livingston.
📋
OFFICIAL GAME PROGRAM
Wyoming vs. BYU · Oct. 11, 1959
PROGRAM
Wyoming Cowboys vs. BYU Cougars — Official Roster & Lineup
Full roster with jersey numbers. AI matched 28 player names to member profiles. Linked to game film on file.
📷

1959 Wyoming Cowboys · Laramie
PHOTO

Facial recognition identified Jim Walden in this photo. No video required — stored in locker automatically.

About Washington State University

Washington State University (WSU) is a public research university in Pullman, Washington. Founded in 1890, WSU competes in the Pac-12 Conference as the Cougars. With a proud athletic tradition spanning over 130 years, WSU sports history is rich with legendary moments waiting to be rediscovered.

130+
Years of History
18
Sports Programs
Pac-12
Conference

About Iowa State University

Iowa State University is a public research university in Ames, Iowa. Founded in 1858, ISU's Cyclones compete in the Big 12 Conference. With decades of storied athletic competition across football, basketball, wrestling, and more, Iowa State's sports legacy is deep and passionate.

About University of Nebraska

The University of Nebraska–Lincoln is a public research university in Lincoln, Nebraska. Founded in 1869, the Cornhuskers compete in the Big Ten Conference and are renowned for one of the most passionate fan bases in college football. Nebraska's volleyball program is also among the nation's most storied.

About Pullman High School

Pullman High School is a public high school in Pullman, Washington, home to the Greyhounds. Located in the heart of the Palouse alongside Washington State University, Pullman HS has a proud athletic tradition competing in the 2A Greater Spokane League.

About University of Notre Dame

The University of Notre Dame is a private Catholic research university in Notre Dame, Indiana. Founded in 1842, the Fighting Irish are one of college football's most storied programs, with 11 consensus national championships and a global fan base. Notre Dame's sports history spans generations of legendary players and unforgettable moments.

About Gonzaga University

Gonzaga University is a private Jesuit university in Spokane, Washington. Founded in 1887, the Bulldogs are nationally renowned for their men's basketball program, qualifying for 25 consecutive NCAA Tournaments. Mark Few has built one of the premier programs in college basketball history at GU.

About Louisiana State University

LSU is a public research university in Baton Rouge, Louisiana. Founded in 1860, the Tigers are one of the SEC's flagship programs with four football national championships and six baseball College World Series titles. Death Valley is widely considered one of the most electric atmospheres in college sports.

4
Football Titles
6
CWS Titles
SEC
Conference

About UCLA

UCLA is a public research university in Los Angeles, California. Founded in 1919, the Bruins are the most decorated program in NCAA history with 119 team national championships. John Wooden's dynasty of 10 NCAA basketball titles in 12 years remains the gold standard of college athletics.

About University of Washington

The University of Washington is a public research university in Seattle, Washington. Founded in 1861, the Huskies compete in the Big Ten Conference. UW has produced over 600 Olympians and its football program has claimed two national championships. Husky Stadium on the shores of Lake Washington is one of college football's most scenic venues.

About University of Texas at Austin

The University of Texas at Austin is a public research university founded in 1883. The Longhorns are one of college sports' most iconic programs, with four football national championships. Darrell K Royal–Texas Memorial Stadium holds over 100,000 fans, and the annual Texas–OU rivalry game is one of the most anticipated in college football.

⚡ GloryDays Partner Program

Build on GloryDays

Connect your platform, broadcast live events, and push content directly into the GloryDays archive. Reach thousands of passionate sports fans and alumni.

Partner Tiers

From simple video embeds to full enterprise white-label deployments

Free
$0 / month
For individuals and small organizations wanting to link content to GloryDays.
  • ✓ Embed GloryDays player widget
  • ✓ Link videos via URL
  • ✓ 10 video submissions/month
  • ✓ GloryDays branding required
  • ✓ Community support
Business
$299 / month
For sports networks, schools, and media companies pushing regular content.
  • ✓ Full REST API access
  • ✓ Unlimited video submissions
  • ✓ Live event streaming embed
  • ✓ Custom partner branding
  • ✓ Webhook notifications
  • ✓ Analytics dashboard
  • ✓ Revenue share on purchases
  • ✓ Priority support
Enterprise
Custom pricing
For universities, state associations, and major sports networks at scale.
  • ✓ White-label school portals
  • ✓ Dedicated subdomain
  • ✓ Custom admin dashboard
  • ✓ SSO / Active Directory
  • ✓ SLA uptime guarantee
  • ✓ Bulk content migration
  • ✓ Dedicated account manager
  • ✓ Custom revenue splits

API Reference

RESTful API · JSON responses · OAuth 2.0 authentication

Endpoints

POST/videos
GET/videos
GET/schools
POST/live-events
GET/members/alerts
POST/webhooks
DEL/videos/:id

POST /v1/videos — Submit a Video

Push a video directly into a school's GloryDays archive. Supports direct file upload or a source URL from YouTube, Vimeo, or Hudl.

POST https://api.glorydays.ai/v1/videos // Headers Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY Content-Type: application/json // Request Body { "school_id": "wsu_cougars", "title": "1997 Rose Bowl Full Game", "year": 1997, "sport": "football", "source_url": "https://youtu.be/abc123", "players": ["Ryan Leaf", "Mike Price"], "visibility": "public", "price": 2.99, "partner_id": "espn_archive" }

Response

{ "status": "success", "video_id": "vid_9f3a2c1b", "url": "https://glorydays.ai/wsu/videos/vid_9f3a2c1b", "alerts_sent": 14, // members notified by name match "status": "processing" }

🏆 Enterprise

Built for the Biggest Names in Sports Media

From ESPN's legacy archive to regional TV networks and state athletic associations — GloryDays provides the community, discovery engine, and monetization layer that large media organizations can't build themselves.

📺 ESPN Sports Network In Discussion
🦊 Fox Sports Sports Network In Discussion
🏈 Hudl Sports Video Partner
🏆 NCAA Governing Body Prospecting
📡 Root Sports Regional Network Partner
🎓 MaxPreps HS Sports Partner
🏛️ State AAs 50 State Assoc. Prospecting
🎬 Getty/AP Archive Stock Footage Prospecting
How Content Licensing Works
1
🤝
License Agreement
GloryDays signs a content licensing deal — either link/embed (safest) or hosted archive (premium). Rights, revenue splits, and takedown terms defined upfront.
2
🔑
API Credentials
Partner receives Enterprise API key, webhook endpoints, and access to the Partner Dashboard. IT team integrates in days, not months.
3
📤
Content Push
Partner pushes video metadata + source URL or file. GloryDays routes it to the right school pages, tags players, sets pricing, and triggers member alerts automatically.
4
👥
Community Amplifies
Members discover, save, share, and purchase. Name alerts bring the players themselves to the platform. Organic reach ESPN's own channels can't buy.
5
💰
Revenue Share
Monthly payouts to the content partner based on views, downloads, and subscription attribution. Full transparency via Partner Dashboard.

📎 Link / Embed Model (Recommended)

Content hosted byPartner (ESPN, YouTube)
GloryDays liabilityMinimal — linking only
Copyright riskLow — partner retains
Takedown processPartner removes source
Revenue modelReferral + ad share
Setup complexitySimple — days to launch

🗄️ Hosted Archive Model (Premium)

Content hosted byGloryDays (AWS S3)
GloryDays liabilityModerate — requires contract
Copyright riskShared — license required
Takedown processDMCA + partner request
Revenue modelDownload sales + subscription
Setup complexityModerate — legal review needed
Example: ESPN Archive Partnership
📺 ESPN — Hypothetical Enterprise Partner

"The Game of the Week" Archive

ESPN holds thousands of hours of college and high school game footage — Rose Bowls, state championships, iconic moments — that generate near-zero revenue sitting in their archive. GloryDays surfaces this content to the exact fans who care about it most.

Via the API, ESPN pushes one video per day. GloryDays auto-routes it to the relevant school page, extracts player names from the metadata, fires name alerts to 14–60 members per video, and makes it purchasable for $2.99–$9.99 per download.

2,400+
Videos / year
~34K
Alerts fired
$180K
Est. annual rev share
📺
ESPN pushes via APITitle, year, players, source URL, price
↓
🏫
Auto-routes to school pageAppears in WSU, ISU, Nebraska archive tabs
↓
🔔
Name alerts fire instantlyTagged players + fans notified by email
↓
💰
Revenue split on purchaseESPN 60% · GloryDays 40%
⚖️
Important for large partners: Major media companies will require formal content licensing agreements — not just API terms of service. GloryDays should engage a sports media attorney before hosting (not just linking) content from ESPN, NCAA, or similar rights holders. The link/embed model is the recommended starting point — it keeps liability with the original rights holder and lets GloryDays scale partnerships quickly without complex legal overhead.
OUR MISSION

Every Game Deserves
to Be Remembered

GloryDays is the first platform built exclusively for amateur sports history — connecting athletes, fans, and schools to the footage, stories, and moments that shaped their lives.

Why We Exist

Amateur sports history is vanishing. Game film sits in attics on deteriorating VHS tapes. Players who gave everything on the field have no way to share their legacy.

📼
Lost Footage
Millions of hours of high school and college game film from the 1920s–2000s exist only on aging VHS, Betamax, and film reels — physically degrading every year. Once they're gone, they're gone forever.
🏠
No Home for Amateur History
YouTube is built for viral content. ESPN archives the pros. But the 1982 Pullman High state championship? The 1994 Iowa State bowl season? There's nowhere for those stories to live — until now.
🔗
No Way to Share the Story
Former players, coaches, and fans hold these memories — but they have no platform to organize, tag, and share them with the teammates and communities who lived them. GloryDays is that platform.
The Untapped Opportunity

Amateur sports is a massive, underserved market — emotionally rich, chronically underfunded for content, and deeply loyal.

🏫
27,000+
High schools with active sports programs in the U.S.
🎓
1,100+
NCAA colleges and universities with varsity athletic programs
👥
50M+
Americans who played high school sports in the last 30 years
📺
$0
Revenue currently generated by most amateur game archives
💾
Est. 200M+
Hours of undigitized amateur sports footage still sitting in homes
Bringing It All Together

GloryDays is more than a video archive — it's a living community built around the games that shaped people's lives.

1
Upload & Preserve
Anyone can upload footage — from digitized VHS to recent smartphone clips. We tag it by school, sport, year, and player names so it's discoverable forever.
2
Find Your Moment
Search by school, player name, sport, or year. Name Alerts notify you the instant footage containing your name is uploaded — even decades later.
3
Connect & Share
Follow school channels, join alumni communities, and share clips with teammates you haven't seen since graduation. The community brings the stories to life.
4
Preserve & Monetize
Content creators earn 80% of every download sale. Schools and media partners earn ongoing revenue from their archives. History pays — when it has a home.

Ready to Preserve Your Legacy?

Join thousands of former athletes, coaches, and fans who are already reliving the games that made them. Upload your first video free — forever.

4
School Portals in Beta

Highlights
From Couch to Pub in 60 Seconds

Open GloryDays on your Roku or Fire TV, search your school, find the game — and watch it with everyone who was there.

📺
1
Open on Any TV
Launch GloryDays on your Roku, Apple TV, Fire TV stick, or Chromecast. Sign in with your existing account.
🏫
2
Find Your School
Search by school name, mascot, or state. Browse by sport, decade, coach, or opponent — all with your TV remote.
📼
3
Pick the Game
Choose from full games, highlight reels, or behind-the-scenes footage. Free 30-second preview before you buy.
🍺
4
Watch Together
Hit play. Share the moment with your old teammates, alumni, or the regulars at the bar who've been telling this story for 40 years.

+12 watching at O'Malley's Bar & Grill

The Story We've
Been Telling for 40 Years

A group of ex-Cougar players walks into a bar. Someone says "remember the '81 Holiday Bowl?" Thirty seconds later it's on the TV. That's GloryDays TV.

Real Use Case · Alumni Night
Six former WSU linemen at O'Malley's. One pulls out his phone, opens GloryDays on the bar's Roku, types "WSU 1981 Holiday Bowl." The whole place is watching in under a minute.
Real Use Case · Reunion Weekend
Iowa State class of '94 reunion at a hotel sports bar. Someone finds the 1994 Big 12 season footage. Players start recognizing themselves on screen.
Real Use Case · Family Watch Night
A daughter finds footage of her father's 1972 Pullman HS state championship on GloryDays TV. First time he's seen himself play in 50 years.

The GloryDays TV interface is designed for couch and remote — simple navigation to find any school, any sport, any year.

Bar & Venue Licensing

Public performance licensing for sports bars, pubs, alumni clubs, and restaurants. Show GloryDays on your screens — legally, affordably, and with full content access.

🍺
Neighborhood Bar
$49 / month
Perfect for local bars and small pubs that want to offer alumni and sports nights.
  • 1 TV / screen license
  • Full GloryDays catalog access
  • Roku or Fire TV app included
  • Schedule alumni viewing nights
  • GloryDays "Now Playing" display
🏟️
Sports Bar
$149 / month
Built for multi-screen sports bars hosting regular alumni and reunion events.
  • Up to 6 TV / screen licenses
  • Full catalog + early access
  • Watch Party mode (group view)
  • Event scheduling tools
  • GloryDays promotional materials
  • Dedicated venue profile page
  • Revenue share on in-venue purchases
🎓
Alumni Club / Hotel
Custom pricing
For university alumni associations, hotel sports bars, and large event venues.
  • Unlimited screens
  • White-label option available
  • Private event streaming
  • School-specific channel curation
  • Dedicated account manager
  • Custom revenue splits

Video title
Send via SMS

Preview link sent!

📼 Upload a Memory

Share an old game film, highlight reel, or sports moment — clips to full 3-hour games. Cloud-stored, no storage limits. You control who sees it.

📁

Click to select a video file

MP4, MOV, AVI, MKV · Up to 50GB per file · Full games welcome

🎤 Voice commands: Say "Title: Pullman 1985 Championship" · "Year: 1985" · "School: Washington State" · "Players: Jim Walden, Bob Smith" — or tap any 🎤 button to fill that field by voice.
🔔 Tag Players & Coaches Triggers Member Alerts
Names you add here are matched against member name alerts. Anyone who has set an alert for a player on this roster will be notified the moment you publish — even if they haven't joined yet.
Names you add will appear here as tags…
🔔 Members with matching name alerts will receive an instant notification when this is published. Pre-registered non-members will get an email invite.

Dave Hoffmann
2 hours ago · WSU Cougars
Just uploaded the full 1981 Holiday Bowl — 48 minutes of pure glory. Rick Johnson was unreal that night. Who else was in the stands? 🏆
❤️ 24 Likes 💬 8 Comments 🔗 Share
🏀
Carol Peterson
Yesterday · Iowa State Cyclones
Does anyone have footage from the 1944 Cyclones season? I found my grandfather's old 8mm reels — going to digitize and upload this week!
❤️ 41 Likes 💬 17 Comments 🔗 Share
🎽
Mike Torres
3 days ago · Pullman HS
Just bought the 1985 Pullman HS State Championship video. Worth every penny. Rueben Mayes in his senior year — absolute legend. 🙌

About University of Notre Dame

The University of Notre Dame is a private Catholic research university in Notre Dame, Indiana. Founded in 1842, the Fighting Irish are one of college football's most storied programs, with 11 consensus national championships and a global fan base. Notre Dame's sports history spans generations of legendary players and unforgettable moments.

11
National Titles
26
Sports Programs
ACC
Conference

About Gonzaga University

Gonzaga University is a private Jesuit university in Spokane, Washington. Founded in 1887, the Bulldogs are nationally renowned for their men's basketball program, qualifying for 25 consecutive NCAA Tournaments. Mark Few has built one of the premier programs in college basketball history at GU.

About Louisiana State University

LSU is a public research university in Baton Rouge, Louisiana. Founded in 1860, the Tigers are one of the SEC's flagship programs with four football national championships and six baseball College World Series titles. Death Valley is widely considered one of the most electric atmospheres in college sports.

About UCLA

UCLA is a public research university in Los Angeles, California. Founded in 1919, the Bruins are the most decorated program in NCAA history with 119 team national championships. John Wooden's dynasty of 10 NCAA basketball titles in 12 years remains the gold standard of college athletics.

About University of Washington

The University of Washington is a public research university in Seattle, Washington. Founded in 1861, the Huskies compete in the Big Ten Conference. UW has produced over 600 Olympians and its football program has claimed two national championships. Husky Stadium on the shores of Lake Washington is one of college football's most scenic venues.

About University of Texas at Austin

The University of Texas at Austin is a public research university founded in 1883. The Longhorns are one of college sports' most iconic programs, with four football national championships. Darrell K Royal–Texas Memorial Stadium holds over 100,000 fans, and the annual Texas–OU rivalry game is one of the most anticipated in college football.

Highlights
From Couch to Pub in 60 Seconds

Open GloryDays on your Roku or Fire TV, search your school, find the game — and watch it with everyone who was there.

📺
1
Open on Any TV
Launch GloryDays on your Roku, Apple TV, Fire TV stick, or Chromecast. Sign in with your existing account.
🏫
2
Find Your School
Search by school name, mascot, or state. Browse by sport, decade, coach, or opponent — all with your TV remote.
📼
3
Pick the Game
Choose from full games, highlight reels, or behind-the-scenes footage. Free 30-second preview before you buy.
🍺
4
Watch Together
Hit play. Share the moment with your old teammates, alumni, or the regulars at the bar who've been telling this story for 40 years.

+12 watching at O'Malley's Bar & Grill

The Story We've
Been Telling for 40 Years

A group of ex-Cougar players walks into a bar. Someone says "remember the '81 Holiday Bowl?" Thirty seconds later it's on the TV. That's GloryDays TV.

Real Use Case · Alumni Night
Six former WSU linemen at O'Malley's. One pulls out his phone, opens GloryDays on the bar's Roku, types "WSU 1981 Holiday Bowl." The whole place is watching in under a minute.
Real Use Case · Reunion Weekend
Iowa State class of '94 reunion at a hotel sports bar. Someone finds the 1994 Big 12 season footage. Players start recognizing themselves on screen.
Real Use Case · Family Watch Night
A daughter finds footage of her father's 1972 Pullman HS state championship on GloryDays TV. First time he's seen himself play in 50 years.

Built for multi-screen sports bars hosting regular alumni and reunion events.
  • Up to 6 TV / screen licenses
  • Full catalog + early access
  • Watch Party mode (group view)
  • Event scheduling tools
  • GloryDays promotional materials
  • Dedicated venue profile page
  • Revenue share on in-venue purchases

Alumni Club / Hotel

For university alumni associations, hotel sports bars, and large event venues.
